M3 - Task 2

Generate Three Custom Visuals Using Canva’s “Text to Image” 

(Estimated time: 60 mins)


Objective: The goal of this activity is to learn how to transform ideas into visuals using text-based image generation. By the end of this task, you’ll understand how the wording of your prompt influences the style, quality, and creativity of the images that Canva produces. This task helps you practice the art of prompting for visuals, a skill that educators can use to design everything from slides and posters to worksheets and digital storyboards.


Instructions:


Step 1: Choose Your Presentation

Open the 10-slide presentation you created earlier in Task 1 (Gamma). Review it carefully and identify three slides that could be enhanced with original visuals. For example:

  • A concept that needs an illustration (e.g., teamwork, space exploration, sustainability)

  • A process or cycle that could be visualized (e.g., water cycle, learning journey)

  • A motivational quote or idea that could use a background image


Step 2: Access Canva’s “Text to Image”

  • Go to Canva.

  • Click Apps → Text to Image.

  • You’ll see a text box where you can describe the image you want.

  • Choose the image style (e.g., Photo, Drawing, Painting, 3D, or Watercolor).


Step 3: Write Your Prompts

Write a clear and specific prompt for each visual. Be detailed about:

  • The subject (what’s in the image)

  • The style (realistic, cartoon, watercolor, etc.)

  • The mood or tone (energetic, peaceful, inspiring, etc.)

  • The context (classroom, nature, lab, space, etc.)


Example prompts:

  • “A watercolor-style illustration of middle school students planting trees in a school garden, sunny and cheerful.”

  • “A realistic image of astronauts teaching science to children inside a space classroom.”

  • “A minimal poster design showing teamwork with teachers and students holding puzzle pieces.”


Step 4: Generate and Refine

  • Click Generate Image. Canva will create multiple image options.

  • Review all results and select the ones that best match your vision.

  • If the results are not what you expected, refine your prompt. Add more descriptive words or adjust the tone.

    • Example: Instead of “students learning,” try “three students sitting in a bright classroom using tablets.”

  • Repeat the process until you have three final visuals that fit your presentation theme.


Step 5: Add the Visuals to Your Presentation

Download your chosen Canva images (or copy them directly into Gamma). Add one image per slide to your presentation.


Position them meaningfully, not just decoratively. Ensure they support the concept or emotion of that slide.


Tip: Try combining Canva visuals with Gamma’s existing design. The blend of AI text (Gamma) and AI images (Canva) often produces professional-quality slides with very little effort.


Step 6: Review and Reflect

Look through your updated presentation.


Ask yourself:

  • Do these visuals make my message clearer or more engaging?

  • Does each image match the tone of the slide?

  • Which of my prompts created the most accurate and creative result?


This reflection helps you learn how to write stronger prompts for AI image generation in the future.


Checklist & Self-Reflection

  • You selected three slides from your Gamma presentation.

  • You wrote three detailed prompts for Canva’s Text to Image.

  • You generated and refined your visuals until satisfied.

  • You integrated the visuals into your existing presentation.

  • You reflected on how prompt quality affected your results.


Self-Reflection Question: Which of your image prompts produced the most effective visual, and what specific words or details made it work so well?


Completion: Once you’ve created your visuals, refined them, and integrated them into your presentation, mark this activity as ‘Complete’ in the LMS.

steps.title: Designing Worksheets and Docs with AI

content.htmlContent:
Designing Worksheets and Docs with AI

Digital Document Creation

In this section, you will learn how to create digital documents directly within Canva using its Magic Write and Magic Media features. These tools help you instantly generate, edit, and visually enhance written content, ideal for creating classroom materials, posters, or study resources in minutes.


You will understand how to:

  • Use Magic Write to generate and refine text content.

  • Modify tone, structure, and conciseness of AI-generated writing.

  • Use Magic Media to create visuals that complement your document.

  • Format, position, and align design elements for a polished final product.


Educators often spend significant time designing learning materials - balancing text, visuals, and formatting. Canva simplifies this process by integrating AI text generation (through Magic Write) and AI image creation (through Magic Media) within a single digital document workspace.


This allows teachers to:

  • Generate educational content instantly.

  • Visually customize text and images for different purposes (e.g., posters, blogs, handouts).

  • Save time while maintaining professional design quality.


Creating a New Canva Document

To start a new Canva Doc:

  • Click on “Docs” in the Canva home interface.

  • A new tab opens with an intuitive layout and a “+” icon for quick actions. This central toolbar provides instant access to features like Magic Write, Magic Design, and formatting options (font, alignment, spacing, etc.).


Using Magic Write for AI-Generated Content

Once the new document opens, use the Magic Write tool, Canva’s AI text generator, by entering the prompt: “Create 10 tips for exam preparation for students.”


Immediately, Magic Write begins generating structured content with clear and relevant points. Users can choose to:

  • Insert the content directly into the document.

  • Modify it using quick commands such as Make it concise or Expand ideas.


This example shows how teachers can quickly produce organized, useful material such as:

  • Study guides

  • Classroom handouts

  • Blog posts or newsletters


Select Make it concise, the AI automatically rewrites long, descriptive text into short, impactful one-liners, perfect for creating a poster-style resource or summary sheet for students.


Editing and Formatting the Content

Once the text is inserted, Canva allows easy editing within the same document.


You can:

  • Adjust the font size and style.

  • Reorder text blocks or bullet points.

  • Add headings and subheadings for clarity.


This stage demonstrates how Magic Write output can be instantly customized without switching between applications.


Integrating Visuals with Magic Media

After generating the written content, the video introduces Magic Media, Canva’s AI-powered image generator.


Type a simple prompt: “Exam preparation”


Magic Media then creates multiple square-format images in concept art style, suitable for use in documents, posters, or blogs.


Teachers can select and insert the image they like best directly into the document.


Example prompts include:

  • “Create an arrow on a bullseye for exam success.” The generated graphic can then be resized, aligned, or repositioned anywhere on the page to complement the written tips visually.


Layout and Design Adjustments 

To control design placement by adjusting alignment and layout:

  • Align center, left, or right to balance the page.

  • Expand to full width for banners or headers.

  • Resize visuals for emphasis or minimalism.


This shows that Canva’s AI tools are not limited to generating content. They help you structure it into visually appealing, shareable formats.


Creating Additional Documents

The session ends with creating another new document using the same process, highlighting that this workflow can be repeated for multiple projects.


This reinforces Canva’s role as a multi-purpose workspace for generating, designing, and organizing classroom or institutional materials.

 
 

Key Takeaways

  • Magic Write generates structured text based on natural language prompts, ideal for lesson tips, notes, or student resources.

  • Instant Editing Commands (like “make it concise”) let you control the tone and length of the text instantly.

  • Magic Media creates customized AI visuals that can be inserted directly into documents.

  • Formatting Controls allow easy resizing, alignment, and layout adjustment to achieve balanced, polished designs.

  • Canva enables educators to merge text creation, design, and formatting within one streamlined workflow.


Now that you’ve seen how to generate digital content and visuals in Canva using Magic Write and Magic Media, the next step is to apply these tools to structured lesson planning.


In the upcoming section, you’ll explore how Canva’s AI can design a complete lesson plan using the 5E instructional model, helping educators build lessons that are engaging, scaffolded, and ready to deliver.

steps.title: Canva AI Powered Student Activity Library

content.htmlContent:
Canva AI Powered Student Activity Library

This section introduces educators to Canva’s AI-powered Student Activity Library, a built-in collection of ready-to-use, customizable activity templates. These templates help teachers create interactive and engaging student activities, from reading comprehension tasks to reflection-based exercises, all in just a few clicks.


Creating classroom activities that truly engage students takes time and planning. Whether it’s designing worksheets, group activities, or reflection prompts, teachers often spend hours formatting documents or searching for inspiration.


Canva’s Education Activity Library simplifies this process. With its AI-supported generator, teachers can instantly create interactive and subject-specific learning activities complete with teacher guides, student instructions, and editable templates, all ready to assign and share.


Accessing the Activity Library

You’ll learn how to navigate Canva’s Education section. From the Home screen, teachers select Education → Lessons → Generate Activities to open Canva’s large Activity Library, filled with pre-made formats for various educational goals.


Types of Activities Available

Canva’s library includes a wide range of activity categories suitable for all grade levels. Examples featured in this session include:

  • Compare and Contrast activities

  • Creative Concept Building

  • Close Reading Sessions

  • Exploring Different Perspectives

  • Guided Research

  • Innovation & Invention challenges

  • Data-Based Decision Making tasks

  • Scenario-Based Reasoning

  • Cause and Effect Diagrams

  • Reflection and Opinion Formation

  • Problem Solving and Planning


These templates are not static; each one can be adapted to your subject, grade level, or lesson theme.


Creating a Sample Activity

An educator creates a comprehension activity on Sustainability.


Steps followed include:

  1. Selecting the Comprehension template from the activity list.

  2. Choosing the grade level (Higher Education) and subject (English Language).

  3. Entering the theme or topic - in this case, Sustainability.


Canva then automatically generates a ready-to-use activity titled Exploring Sustainability: A Learning Activity, which includes:

  • A Teacher Guide explaining how to conduct the activity.

  • A structured layout beginning with an Introduction, followed by Tips for Success, Vocabulary Review, Comprehension Questions, and Creative Extension Tasks (like designing a poster).


Teacher & Student Views 

The teacher can preview both perspectives:

  • Teacher View: Includes editable sections, guidance, and resource upload options.

  • Student View: Shows only the activity and answer spaces, keeping instructions hidden to ensure independent engagement.


Adding Another Activity to the Same Lesson

You’ll see how to add multiple activities under one lesson plan. For example, combining a comprehension exercise with a reflection activity to extend learning.


By selecting “Reflection” from the activity library and keeping the same grade level, Canva automatically generates a new reflection task, designed to deepen learning and encourage student self-evaluation.


The Teacher Guide and Interactive Whiteboard View 

Each activity includes a Teacher Guide, a crucial feature that:

  • Explains the learning goals and steps to implement the activity.

  • Highlights what to delete before sharing (for example, teacher-only notes).

  • Offers a “Quick Recall” summary and recommended structure for facilitation.


Some activities open in a Whiteboard format, allowing for interactive collaboration. Here, teachers and students can use digital sticky notes, draw connections, or fill in sections like “Helpful Actions,” “Missed Opportunities,” or “SMART Goals.”


Assigning the Activity

Once finalized, teachers can assign the activity to students directly through Canva.


The platform allows for:

  • Reviewing the content for alignment with learning goals.

  • Uploading supporting resources (such as presentations, links, or videos).

  • Checking sample answers.

  • Sharing the completed template with students for submission or classroom use.

 
 

Key Takeaways

  • AI-Supported Activity Generation: Canva’s Education section automatically generates classroom activities from simple prompts, saving teachers preparation time.

  • Diverse Learning Formats: Activities include comprehension, reflection, problem-solving, and data-based reasoning tasks suitable for different disciplines.

  • Built-In Teacher Guides: Each activity provides clear facilitation notes, usage steps, and learning objectives.

  • Interactive Whiteboard Format: Activities can be visually modified and shared digitally, supporting active, hands-on learning.

  • Seamless Assignment & Sharing: Teachers can preview, customize, and assign activities directly within Canva, making it ideal for hybrid and online teaching environments.


Now that you’ve seen how Canva’s AI generates ready-made activities complete with teacher guides and student templates, it’s time to go a step further.


In the next section, you’ll learn how to use Canva’s AI-powered education tools to create custom student activities and generate lesson content directly inside Canva Documents.

Student Activity Plan

In this section, you’ll learn how to use Canva’s AI-powered education tools to create custom student activities and generate lesson content directly inside Canva Documents. You will also see how to refine and format AI-generated text for educational use.


By the end, you’ll understand how Canva helps educators move from prompt to polished classroom material, whether it’s a student worksheet, a group project guide, or written instructional content.


Educators often juggle multiple tasks: designing engaging student activities, creating clear instructional text, and formatting it visually for classroom use. Canva brings these processes together under one platform by combining AI text generation (Magic Write) with activity templates that include built-in teacher guides and student instructions.


Generating Student Activities

Explore Canva’s Education section and use the AI to generate a new activity for students.


For example, a teacher creates an activity for a 12th-grade Art class on the topic Elements and Principles of Design.


The prompt given includes all the key details:

“Create a group activity for 12th grade students on the elements and principles of design. Duration: 30 minutes.”


The AI then generates a student activity plan, which includes:

  • Learning Outcomes: Students identify, discuss, and apply design elements (like line, shape, texture, balance, and contrast).

  • Application Task: Students collaboratively create a group composition that demonstrates intentional use of these concepts.


The system also allows the teacher to choose from different activity types, such as Apply, Compare, or Expand, depending on the desired cognitive level or skill outcome.


Teacher Guide and Student Instructions 

Each generated activity includes a Teacher Guide, which provides:

  • Guidance on how to review and prepare the content.

  • A suggested lesson flow (introduction, reading, discussion, and task).

  • Prompts for teachers to check sample answers and share templates with students.


Teachers are also reminded to delete instructional notes before sharing the activity with the class.


The student section includes creative and open-ended prompts such as:

  • “Explain how you can use contrast between light and dark.”

  • “Create a short video showing how elements and principles of design can be demonstrated differently.”


This structured activity ensures both theoretical understanding and creative application, perfect for classroom or group learning settings.


Using Magic Write to Generate Lesson Text

You’ll see how Magic Write, Canva’s AI text generator, helps teachers quickly create written content for educational documents.


Example:

“Write a 100-word introduction about the importance of time management for college students.”


Once the text is generated, Canva allows the user to refine it using contextual commands such as:

  • Continue writing: To expand the passage or add details.

  • Make this more formal: To adjust tone and academic style.

  • Add a real-life example: To make content relatable and practical.


After applying these refinements, the final text includes:

  • A clear structure with an introduction and conclusion.

  • A real-life example (e.g., a student balancing study and part-time work).

  • A formal tone suitable for classroom handouts or assignments.


Designing Documents with the Generated Text

The improved content can then be directly inserted into a Canva document for design and layout.


Educators can:

  • Format the text visually (add color, boxes, or sections).

  • Combine written material with AI-generated activity templates.

  • Create polished, ready-to-print lesson sheets or student handouts, all without leaving Canva.


This integration makes Canva a one-stop solution for both content creation and design presentation.

 
 

Key Takeaways

  • AI-Powered Student Activities: Canva generates ready-to-teach classroom activities with built-in learning outcomes, student instructions, and teacher guides.

  • Customization Flexibility: Teachers can modify the activity focus (e.g., Apply, Compare, or Expand) to fit their lesson goals.

  • Magic Write for Text Generation: Educators can instantly create and refine educational text using commands like “Continue writing”, “Make this formal”, and “Add examples.”

  • Integrated Workflow: Canva allows teachers to generate, edit, and visually design their documents within the same workspace.

  • Creative Outputs: AI-generated text and visuals can be combined to produce worksheets, reflection prompts, group projects, or informational documents.


Now that you’ve learned how Canva’s AI can generate both activities and written content, the next step is to make your lessons visually engaging.


In the upcoming section, you’ll explore Visual Infographic Creation, where Canva’s AI tools help you turn information and data into dynamic, easy-to-understand classroom visuals that make learning more memorable.

Create Worksheets and Infographics

Now that you can generate text using Magic Write, it’s time to make it visually appealing and easy to use in class.


Think of how often you design worksheets or infographics for students,  whether it’s a marketing quiz, a communication exercise, or a business model summary. These materials often need clear formatting and visuals that guide learning.


In this section, you’ll learn:

  • How to use Canva Docs templates to turn AI-generated text into organized worksheets.

  • How to create infographics that summarize complex concepts visually.


By the end, you’ll be able to create visually engaging resources that help students learn better, and enjoy the process.


Creating a Worksheet from Magic Write Text 

We open a Canva Doc and type:“/Magic Write → Create a 5-question worksheet on workplace communication for management students.”


Within seconds, AI generates five well-framed questions.


We format the output using headers, dividers, and spacing. Canva’s document templates allow us to add icons, lines for responses, and branding (like course name and logo).


Turning Text into Visual Designs

We enter the prompt in Canva AI and click “design for me”


Canva automatically places the text into a well-balanced visual layout that looks like a poster or digital worksheet.


Creating an Infographic from AI Text

“Summarize five key stress management techniques for college students.”

 

Now we can customise it to add color schemes, icons, and charts.


Examples

  • Business students: “The 5Ps of Marketing”

  • Psychology students: “Maslow’s Hierarchy of Needs”

  • Economics students: “Circular Flow of Income”


Each infographic can be completed in minutes, combining accurate text with appealing visuals.


Design Tips for Educators

  • Keep one idea per slide or visual section.

  • Use consistent fonts and colors.

  • Include brief text with strong visuals — less clutter means better retention.

 
 

Now you’ve seen how to turn AI-generated text into visually engaging worksheets and infographics. Your materials no longer need to be plain text. They can now teach visually.


In the next video, we’ll focus on communication, specifically, how to create parent newsletters using Magic Write. You’ll see how AI can help you craft warm, professional, and informative updates that keep families connected to what’s happening in your classroom.

Create Worksheets and Infographics

Now that you can generate text using Magic Write, it’s time to make it visually appealing and easy to use in class.


Think of how often you design worksheets or infographics for students,  whether it’s a marketing quiz, a communication exercise, or a business model summary. These materials often need clear formatting and visuals that guide learning.


In this section, you’ll learn:

  • How to use Canva Docs templates to turn AI-generated text into organized worksheets.

  • How to create infographics that summarize complex concepts visually.


By the end, you’ll be able to create visually engaging resources that help students learn better, and enjoy the process.


Creating a Worksheet from Magic Write Text 

We open a Canva Doc and type:“/Magic Write → Create a 5-question worksheet on workplace communication for management students.”


Within seconds, AI generates five well-framed questions.


We format the output using headers, dividers, and spacing. Canva’s document templates allow us to add icons, lines for responses, and branding (like course name and logo).


Turning Text into Visual Designs

We enter the prompt in Canva AI and click “design for me”


Canva automatically places the text into a well-balanced visual layout that looks like a poster or digital worksheet.


Creating an Infographic from AI Text

“Summarize five key stress management techniques for college students.”

 

Now we can customise it to add color schemes, icons, and charts.


Examples

  • Business students: “The 5Ps of Marketing”

  • Psychology students: “Maslow’s Hierarchy of Needs”

  • Economics students: “Circular Flow of Income”


Each infographic can be completed in minutes, combining accurate text with appealing visuals.


Design Tips for Educators

  • Keep one idea per slide or visual section.

  • Use consistent fonts and colors.

  • Include brief text with strong visuals — less clutter means better retention.

 
 

Now you’ve seen how to turn AI-generated text into visually engaging worksheets and infographics. Your materials no longer need to be plain text. They can now teach visually.


In the next video, we’ll focus on communication, specifically, how to create parent newsletters using Magic Write. You’ll see how AI can help you craft warm, professional, and informative updates that keep families connected to what’s happening in your classroom.

steps.title: Combining AI Text with AI Images

content.htmlContent:
Combining AI Text with AI Images

You’ve learned how to generate AI text with Magic Write and how to design visuals using Text to Image. Now, let’s bring both worlds together.


In this section, you’ll learn how to combine AI text and AI visuals into a single, cohesive document, such as a one-page learning resource, project summary, or student handout.


We’ll focus on:

  • Structuring your document for clarity and flow.

  • Pairing visuals and text meaningfully.

  • Exporting and sharing your final designs.


By the end, you’ll be able to create professional, fully AI-assisted teaching materials that look like they were designed by a creative studio.


Combining AI Text and Images

We start by opening a new Canva Doc.

Step 1: Use Magic Write to generate text on “Top 5 Digital Marketing Trends for 2025.”

Step 2: Use Canva’s Text to Image to generate complementary visuals, such as “a 3D-style illustration of data analytics on a laptop screen.”


Arranging the Document Layout

We place visuals beside text to create balance.


Sections include:

  • Header with title

  • Two-column layout (text + image)

  • Footer with teacher or course name


Color and Typography Choices

We choose consistent colors and readable fonts.AI-generated visuals are resized and aligned to match the tone — professional for higher ed, playful for creative classes.


Finishing Touches

We add line breaks, icons, and headings to structure the content clearly. Canva Docs automatically aligns everything for a clean, professional finish.


Export and Sharing Options

Once complete, we export the document as a PDF for sharing or interactive link for digital viewing. The final product can be uploaded to the LMS, printed, or shared in class.


You’ve now mastered the full workflow of document design with AI, from generating text and visuals to creating finished, polished materials ready for use.


With Canva Docs, Magic Write, and Text to Image, you can create worksheets, infographics, and newsletters that look professional and are personalized for your students.


In the next module, you’ll explore how educators are creating videos and voice content with AI.

M3 - Task 3

Create a One-Page Student Worksheet or Parent Newsletter Using Magic Write 

(Estimated time: 90 mins)


Objective: The goal of this activity is to create a real, classroom-ready document, either a student worksheet or a parent newsletter, using AI-assisted writing and design. By doing this, you’ll learn how to merge AI writing (Magic Write) and AI visuals (Text to Image) into a cohesive, professional-looking document that saves you time and amplifies your communication impact.


Instructions:


Step 1: Choose Your Format

Decide what kind of document you want to create. You can pick one of the following:

  • Option A: Student Worksheet

    Create an engaging one-page worksheet related to a topic you teach. For example: “Understanding the Water Cycle,” “Grammar Challenge: Common Nouns,” or “Exploring Renewable Energy.”

  • Option B: Parent Newsletter

    Design a short newsletter update for parents. For example: “Highlights from Science Week,” “Upcoming Field Trip to the Museum,” or “Tips for Supporting Reading at Home.”


Step 2: Set Up Your Document in Canva

  • Go to Canva.

  • Search for “Worksheet” or “Newsletter” in the template bar.

  • Choose a layout that fits your purpose — simple, clear, and one-page.

  • Open the template and rename your file (e.g., “AI-Enhanced Worksheet” or “Parent Update October 2025”).


Step 3: Use Magic Write to Generate Your Content

  • In the text box or side panel, click “Magic Write.”

  • Type a specific prompt describing what you want AI to write. Include details like topic, tone, and target audience. Example prompts:

    • “Write a 5-question activity sheet for Grade 7 students about the importance of nutrition. Include short definitions and a small quiz.”

    • “Write a 150-word parent newsletter update about the upcoming Science Fair, highlighting student preparation and excitement.”

  • Review the AI-generated content.

  • Edit or personalize it; add instructions, your name, class details, or school logo.


Step 4: Add an AI Image You Generated Earlier

Use at least one of the images you created in Task 2 (Canva Images). You can:

  • Add it as the main visual on your worksheet (e.g., a header image or activity illustration).

  • Place it in your newsletter as a photo section or feature image.


If needed, you can use Magic Eraser to clean up the image or Magic Animate to add light motion for digital use.


Step 5: Design and Format Your Page

  • Use Magic Design to auto-style your layout.

  • Adjust colors and fonts to match your classroom or school branding.

  • Add headings like:

    • For Worksheets: “Activity: Let’s Explore!”, “Think and Reflect,” “Bonus Challenge”

    • For Newsletters: “From the Teacher’s Desk,” “Upcoming Events,” “Student Spotlight”

  • Keep your design clean and balanced. Use white space wisely.

  • Check text readability and ensure all AI elements (text + images) support your learning or communication goal.


Step 6: Final Review

Before saving your final version, ask yourself:

  • Is my message clear and appropriate for my audience?

  • Does the layout look professional and easy to read?

  • Do the visuals complement the text meaningfully?

  • Is the tone warm, positive, and aligned with school communication standards?


Make small edits where needed — shorten paragraphs, fix spacing, and align images neatly.


Step 7: Export Your Document

Once complete:

  • Click Share → Download → PDF (Standard) for digital use, or Print (High Quality) if you plan to distribute it in class.

  • You may also copy the link to your design and upload it to your LMS or school communication portal.


Checklist & Self-Reflection

  • You selected a format (Worksheet or Newsletter).

  • You used Magic Write to generate AI text content.

  • You incorporated at least one AI-generated image from Task 2.

  • You used Magic Design or custom formatting for layout.

  • You reviewed and refined your final page for clarity and appeal.


Self-Reflection Question: How did combining Magic Write (for text) and Text to Image (for visuals) change your creative process compared to designing from scratch? Reflect on whether the AI tools made your workflow easier, and how you might use them for future classroom materials.


Completion: Once you’ve completed and reviewed your worksheet or newsletter, mark this activity as ‘Complete’ in the LMS.

title: M4: The Next Frontier: AI Video & Audio

content.htmlContent:
M4 - Learning Objectives

By the end of this module, educators will be able to:

  • Navigate Runway’s creative suite and apply basic editing tools to create short video clips.

  • Write effective prompts for video generation, ensuring visuals align with lesson goals and student comprehension levels.

  • Explore and compare the capabilities of Google’s Veo and OpenAI’s Sora to identify the right platform for different classroom storytelling needs.

  • Use free text-to-speech tools to convert written content (e.g., reading passages or announcements) into clear audio files.

  • Develop short educational podcasts, combining AI-generated narration and background elements to support flipped classroom or revision activities.

Creative Video Clips with RunwayML

Introduction to Runway's Creative Suite

Welcome to RunwayML, your AI-powered creative film studio!


Imagine if your lesson could be its own movie trailer. How would you hook students in the first 10 seconds? Could you show photosynthesis as a glowing forest, or historical events as epic cinematic sequences?


Runway’s Creative Suite allows you to do exactly that: turn your imagination into short AI-generated clips that move, shine, and tell a story.

  • How could motion visuals make abstract lessons tangible?

  • Which subjects or lessons in your curriculum could benefit from cinematic storytelling?

  • Could visuals help multilingual learners or students who struggle with reading comprehension?


Key Features:

  1. Text-to-Video: Transform any text prompt into moving visuals.

  2. Infinite Image: Expand any image into immersive backgrounds for lessons.

  3. Motion Brush: Animate objects, people, or natural phenomena within a scene.

  4. Templates & Presets: Quickly create professional-quality videos without technical expertise.


Classroom Use Cases:

  • Biology: Animate the life cycle of a butterfly with motion and color transitions.

  • History: Show the construction of the Great Wall or the Renaissance cityscape evolving over time.

  • Literature: Visualize Shakespeare’s A Midsummer Night’s Dream with forest sprites and moonlight.

  • Mathematics: Illustrate complex geometry concepts like the formation of fractals or 3D solids.

 
 

You’ve seen how RunwayML can turn your imagination into moving images.


Reflection Prompts:

  • How could cinematic visuals improve student engagement and memory retention?

  • What subject areas in your curriculum are best suited for motion-based storytelling?


Now let’s try working on RunwayML

Write one sentence describing your lesson as a cinematic trailer.


Examples:

  • “A meteor shower streaking across the night sky to illustrate the wonders of astronomy.”


“Raindrops falling on leaves, showing the journey of water through the ecosystem.”

steps.title: Best Practices for Prompting Short Video Clips

content.htmlContent:
Best Practices for Prompting Short Video Clips

Here’s where the magic truly comes alive.


Text-to-Video in Runway allows you to type ideas and see them animated instantly. No cameras, no green screens, just imagination.



Think about a concept from your next lesson that could benefit from a visual representation. 


Examples:

  • “A glowing neuron firing in the brain” (biology)

  • “The Industrial Revolution unfolding in a cityscape” (history)

  • “Shapes building a city skyline” (geometry)


Real Classroom Use Cases:

  1. Science Experiments: Show chemical reactions, plant growth, or planetary orbits in motion.

  2. Language Learning: Generate animated scenarios for dialogues, cultural contexts, or vocabulary practice.

  3. History & Social Studies: Bring historical events to life:  e.g., the Moon landing, ancient civilizations, or key battles.

  4. Art & Music: Visualize abstract concepts like musical rhythms, color theory, or design patterns.


Reflection Prompts:

  • Which lessons could benefit most from animated visual explanations?

  • Could motion visuals support special education or multilingual students in understanding concepts?


Try-It Activity:

  1. Pick a lesson topic.

  2. Write a short text prompt (1-2 sentences) describing the scene.

  3. Generate a video using RunwayML.

  4. Ask students to write a short reflection: “What did I notice in the animation that I hadn’t before?”

steps.title: Exploring Image-to-Video and Infinite Image Expansion

content.htmlContent:
Exploring Image-to-Video and Infinite Image Expansion

Every teacher is a storyteller and every lesson you teach has a plot. There’s a beginning where curiosity sparks, a middle where discovery unfolds, and an end where understanding takes root.


Now, imagine if your lessons could be told not just with words and slides but with moving visuals, color, and emotion. With RunwayML, you can bring your stories to life as short cinematic videos transforming even the simplest classroom topic into a visual journey your students will remember long after the lesson ends.


Think:

  • What stories are already hidden within your curriculum?

  • Could a lesson on “climate change” become a story about resilience and recovery?

  • Could your “algebra lesson” become a journey from confusion to clarity?

  • How could color, pacing, and movement help communicate not just facts but feelings?


Storytelling through visuals helps bridge the gap between imagination and understanding. It invites students to connect emotionally with what they learn, not just memorize it.


Here’s how you can start using RunwayML’s Image-to-Video feature in a few easy steps:

  • Create an Account: Go to the RunwayML website and sign up if you’re new.

  • Upload Your Images: Pick the pictures you want to turn into a video. They can be photos, drawings, or digital art.

  • Choose the Tool: Open the Image-to-Video option on RunwayML and select the model you’d like to use.

  • Adjust the Settings: Change the speed, style, and effects to make the video look the way you want.

  • Create the Video: Click Generate and let RunwayML’s AI transform your images into a short video.

  • Save and Share: When it’s done, download your video and share it wherever you want!



Real-World Examples for Educators:

  • History as a Living Narrative: Bring historical moments to life through AI storytelling. For example, a teacher creates a 2-minute AI-generated film showing the rise and fall of the Roman Empire soldiers marching, cities expanding, maps shifting over time  all narrated by an AI avatar from Module 4.1. Students can “see” the transformation of civilizations rather than just read about them, making timelines come alive with motion and emotion.

  • Math as a Visual Problem-Solving Story: Instead of equations on a board, imagine showing a basketball’s arc representing a quadratic function, or a bridge being built to demonstrate geometry in engineering. The video becomes a narrative of problem-solving: “The challenge -> the formula -> the solution.” This approach helps students visualize math in real contexts and understand why it matters.

  • Literature as Cinematic Poetry: Bring imagination into your literature class! Picture a clip visualizing a poem , where the line “The moonlight danced on silver waves” literally comes alive as shimmering moonlight moving over a vast sea. Students can create their own interpretations by describing how imagery and tone would look if it were a short film. This helps bridge textual analysis with creative expression.

  • Environmental Science as a Cycle of Change: Show the story of a forest from lush green life, to deforestation, to regeneration. Through motion, students can “see” the environmental consequences of human actions, making sustainability topics personal and urgent. A short AI-generated video could complement data charts and scientific discussions, inspiring empathy and deeper ecological understanding.

  • Global Classrooms, Shared Stories: Teachers across countries can collaborate to create “Global Story Exchanges.” For example, a school in India and another in Spain each create short AI clips about “Water in Our World.” Both classes share their visual stories one focusing on monsoon rains, the other on drought and conservation sparking cultural exchange and empathy through storytelling.


You’ve just seen how AI video storytelling can make lessons vivid, emotional, and memorable. Stories stay with students because they connect facts to feelings and when learning feels like a story, it’s remembered for life.


Reflection Prompts:

  • Which lessons could you tell as a story with characters, challenges, and discoveries?

  • How might this storytelling approach help students see learning as a journey instead of isolated topics?

  • Could this help students who struggle with traditional learning methods visualize concepts more easily?


Try-It Classroom Activity:

Your Challenge: Turn one of your upcoming lessons into a mini AI film.

  1. Choose a topic. (Example: The Life Cycle of a Star, The French Revolution, Photosynthesis, or The Pythagorean Theorem.)

  2. Break it into three acts:

    • Beginning: Introduce curiosity or question: “How do stars come to life?”

    • Middle: Show the transformation: “A cloud of gas ignites into a blazing star.”

    • End: Reveal the understanding: “Stars die, but their elements form new worlds.”

  3. Write short prompts (1-2 lines each) describing what visuals should appear in each act.

  4. Generate each clip using RunwayML.

  5. Combine the clips into a complete short film (60-90 seconds).


Optional Add-On: Ask your students to respond with a 3-line reflection: “What part of the video helped you understand the topic best  and why?”


This helps them connect emotionally and cognitively to the visual story.


Extension Ideas for Creative Educators:

  1. Voice + Video Fusion: Use Text-to-Speech to add narration to your RunwayML videos  like a guide explaining the journey. Example: “Watch as the Earth heals itself after deforestation.

  2. Prompt Writing Practice: Use Notion AI  to co-create video prompts with your students. Ask them to refine the language until the AI produces the most fitting visuals.

  3. Student Storytelling Projects: Let students create their own AI story videos as projects for instance:

    • A history student tells the story of an unsung hero.

    • A science student visualizes the water cycle as an adventure.

    • A language student creates a dialogue video in another language.

  4. Cultural Exchange through Storytelling: Partner with another classroom abroad. Exchange AI-generated story clips about local festivals, traditions, or historical events. Students can compare visual interpretations and discuss how storytelling transcends language barriers.

  5. AI Film Festival in Class!: Host a mini “AI Story Showcase” day where students present their short videos. Categories could include: Most Inspiring Visual, Best Lesson Connection, Creative Prompt Writing, or Most Emotional Storyline. This not only boosts engagement but also celebrates creativity and digital literacy.

M4 - Task 1

Create a 5-Second Conceptual Video Clip with RunwayML

(Estimated Time: 60 minutes)


Objective: This task allows you to experience how AI-generated motion can transform the way you introduce topics in your classroom. You will use RunwayML, an AI video creation tool, to generate a short 5-second conceptual video clip that serves as a lesson hook, something that sparks curiosity and sets the tone for your lesson. By the end of this activity, you’ll learn how to transform a single idea or image into a visually dynamic video that captures attention within seconds.


What You’ll Create:

A short, 5-second conceptual video clip that visually introduces or teases the topic of a lesson, such as:

  • “The evolution of technology” for a business class

  • “Photosynthesis in action” for a biology lecture

  • “The energy of a busy trading floor” for an economics unit

  • “The Industrial Revolution begins” for a history module


Think of it as your video version of a classroom icebreaker; short, striking, and memorable.


Instructions


Step 1: Choose a Lesson Concept or Topic

Select one concept or lesson idea that could benefit from a short visual introduction.


Examples:

  • “Artificial Intelligence in daily life”

  • “The water cycle”

  • “Leadership under pressure”

  • “Social media and human connection”


Keep it visual and action-oriented; something that can be expressed through movement or emotion.


Step 2: Access RunwayML

  • Visit https://runwayml.com 

  • Sign up for a free or trial account.

  • Click “Gen-2” (Runway’s text-to-video or image-to-video model).


You’ll see options to:

  • Type a text prompt, or

  • Upload an image and animate it.


Step 3: Write Your Prompt (or Upload an Image)

If using Text-to-Video, type a short, descriptive prompt.


Examples:

  • “A futuristic classroom glowing with blue AI interfaces, cinematic lighting.”

  • “A drop of water forming into a cloud and raining over green trees, macro shot.”

  • “Students brainstorming ideas on sticky notes in a modern workspace, warm lighting.”


If using Image-to-Video, upload an image from Canva or Pixabay and type a motion cue like: “Add slow zoom-in and glowing animation around key elements.”


Tip: Keep prompts under 25 words. Use action verbs like “zoom in,” “pan across,” “fade through,” or “move toward.”


Step 4: Adjust Video Settings

  • Duration: Set to around 5 seconds.

  • Motion Style: Cinematic or smooth camera motion.

  • Quality: Standard or High (depending on plan).Preview before generating.


Step 5: Generate & Refine

Click “Generate” and wait a few seconds.


Watch your clip carefully:

  • Does it visually represent your topic?

  • Does it feel smooth and engaging?If not, tweak the prompt slightly (change lighting, movement, or description) and re-generate.


Step 6: Save & Use the Clip

  • Download the final 5-second video.

  • Add it to a presentation, use it as your lecture opener, or embed it in your LMS as a visual teaser.Example use: “Play before class begins to set the mood for the topic.”


Checklist

  • I selected a single, visual classroom concept.

  • I wrote a clear, descriptive text prompt (under 25 words).

  • I generated at least one short video clip using RunwayML.

  • I previewed, refined, and downloaded the best version.

  • I identified where this clip can be used in my lesson plan (as a hook, background, or transition).


Creative Tips

  • Think Cinematically: Imagine you’re creating a movie trailer for your lesson. What would it look like?

  • Use Movement Words: AI responds well to verbs like “zoom,” “pan,” “fade,” “rotate.”

  • Match Mood to Subject: Bright, colorful clips for creative topics; darker or subtle tones for serious discussions.

  • Keep It Short: A strong 5-second visual is more powerful than a long clip with too much detail.


Self-Reflection Questions

Reflect on the following after completing your task:

  1. How did creating a visual “hook” change the way you think about lesson openings?

  2. Did the AI-generated clip match your mental image of the concept? Why or why not?

  3. How could you use RunwayML or similar tools for student-led creativity (e.g., project intros, storytelling, or digital art)?

  4. What limitations did you notice in the tool, and how would you adapt your prompt to improve results next time?


Completion

Once you’ve created and downloaded your 5-second clip, share it or store it in your teaching resources folder.


You can also combine it with music or narration later to build a full AI-driven lesson introduction.


When finished, mark this activity as “Complete” in your LMS.

steps.title: A Glimpse into the Future: Veo & Sora

content.htmlContent:
A Glimpse into the Future: Veo & Sora

Exploring Google’s Veo

Let’s imagine this together. You type a simple line: “A teacher walking through space explaining gravity to her students.” Seconds later, an entire cinematic video appears floating astronauts, swirling galaxies, glowing stars, all beautifully shot with realistic lighting and camera motion.


No cameras. No film crew. No green screens.  Just your imagination, turned into moving art.


That’s the kind of creativity Google’s Veo brings to life! 


Veo represents a giant leap forward in AI video generation, it’s not just a tool, it’s a storytelling companion. It understands your text, your emotions, your descriptions, and then builds scenes that feel alive complete with dynamic movement, realistic characters, and even mood-based lighting.


What if every lesson you teach could feel like a short film visual, immersive, and full of wonder?


How might this kind of visual storytelling change the way students see learning?


In this section, we’ll explore how Google Veo is pushing the boundaries of AI-generated video. It’s designed not just for professional creators but also for teachers, students, and innovators who want to communicate complex ideas visually without needing cameras or editing software.


Veo’s capabilities go beyond static visuals; it captures the emotion and flow of a scene. It understands context like “a calm morning in a forest” versus “a stormy battlefield” and generates motion, color, and light that match your story’s tone.


This means that your lessons can now have cinematic storytelling power, your words become motion, your ideas become experiences.


​​Key Aspect: Advanced Capabilities

  • Cinematic Control: Users can specify camera angles ("close-up," "aerial shot," "tracking shot"), lighting ("golden hour," "moody lighting"), and visual styles ("photorealistic," "animated," "vintage film").

  • Image-to-Video: It can animate a static image, bringing source material like a diagram, historical photo, or AI-generated image to life.

  • Video Editing: It can extend existing video clips or create seamless transitions between different scenes.

  • Audio Generation: Newer versions can generate synchronized audio, sound effects, and even realistic dialogue based on the prompt.


So get ready to step into the future of visual learning, where teaching and creativity blend seamlessly into immersive storytelling powered by AI. 


Use Case: Visualizing the "Unseeable"

  • Concept: Make abstract, complex, or invisible concepts tangible.

  • Examples:

    • STEM: Show the process of protein folding, visualize a chemical reaction at the molecular level, or generate a simulation of a black hole.

    • Humanities: Create a video of an abstract philosophical concept, like "Plato's Cave."

    • Economics: Animate a graph to show supply and demand shifting in real-time.


Use Case: Dynamic Content & "Micro-Learning"

  • Concept: Quickly create engaging, short-form video content to supplement lectures or readings.

  • Examples:

    • All Disciplines: Generate a 60-second "explainer video" for a key term or theory.

    • Online Learning: Create visually interesting video introductions for weekly modules.

    • Marketing: (For the institution) Create short, dynamic videos for course promotion on social media.


Use Case: Recreating Scenarios & Simulations

  • Concept: Provide students with a visual context that is impossible or expensive to replicate.

  • Examples:

    • History: Generate a "realistic" short scene of a specific historical event, like a debate in the Roman Senate or a 1920s street scene.

    • Business: Create a short case study video depicting a difficult customer interaction or a boardroom negotiation.

    • Medicine/Nursing: Generate a video simulating the visual onset of specific patient symptoms.


Use Case: Empowering Student Creation

  • Concept: Lower the technical barrier for students to create high-quality video-based assignments, focusing them on content and critical thinking instead of complex video editing.

  • Examples:

    • Literature: Students generate short "trailers" for the novel they are studying, capturing its tone and themes.

    • Languages: Students create a short, subtitled video dialogue between two AI-generated characters in the target language.

    • All Disciplines: As an alternative to a standard presentation, students create a 3-minute video essay or digital story illustrating their research.

 
 

You’ve just witnessed the incredible potential of Google’s Veo, an AI that doesn’t just generate clips but tells stories with cinematic depth.


Unlike older AI tools that produced short, grainy, or robotic motion videos, Veo can craft smooth, realistic scenes that look and feel like professional productions. It’s as if you suddenly had an entire film crew working for your classroom! 


Here’s what makes Veo so revolutionary for educators and creators alike:


Why Veo Matters in Education and Creativity? 


From Ideas to Immersive Learning

Teachers can instantly turn abstract ideas into visual journeys.


Imagine saying: “A camera zooms into a drop of water to show microscopic life inside.”


Veo brings that sentence to life. Students see what they’re learning instead of just reading about it. This bridges the gap between imagination and understanding.


A Storytelling Boost for Every Subject

Every subject has a story and Veo helps tell it beautifully:

  • Science: “A tour inside a volcano as lava flows and reshapes the earth.”

  • History: “A cinematic retelling of the signing of the Indian Constitution.”

  • Geography: “A journey through the monsoon season across Asia.”

  • Art & Literature: “A surreal interpretation of Shakespeare’s A Midsummer Night’s Dream.”


Now, every topic becomes a visual adventure.


Empowering Students as Creators

Instead of passively watching, students become AI filmmakers. They can create short documentaries, visual essays, or concept stories with no cameras required. This fosters creativity, collaboration, and digital literacy core 21st-century skills.


Accessibility and Inclusion

Veo makes visual learning accessible for diverse learners including those who grasp concepts better through visual storytelling rather than text-heavy explanations. Students learning new languages, for example, can use Veo-generated clips to visualize vocabulary and cultural contexts.


Classroom Use Cases: Turning Lessons into Cinematic Stories

  • Science: “A Journey Through the Human Body”

    Type: “A microscopic camera flying through veins, showing red blood cells moving in rhythm.”The result? A mesmerizing visual that turns biology into an adventure inside the human body.

  • Literature: “Reimagining Shakespeare”

    Prompt: “Two figures walking in a foggy London street as poetic narration plays in the background.”Students can analyze tone, mood, and setting visually bringing literature to life.

  • Geography: “The Birth of a River”

Exploring Google’s Veo

Let’s imagine this together. You type a simple line: “A teacher walking through space explaining gravity to her students.” Seconds later, an entire cinematic video appears floating astronauts, swirling galaxies, glowing stars, all beautifully shot with realistic lighting and camera motion.


No cameras. No film crew. No green screens.  Just your imagination, turned into moving art.


That’s the kind of creativity Google’s Veo brings to life! 


Veo represents a giant leap forward in AI video generation, it’s not just a tool, it’s a storytelling companion. It understands your text, your emotions, your descriptions, and then builds scenes that feel alive complete with dynamic movement, realistic characters, and even mood-based lighting.


What if every lesson you teach could feel like a short film visual, immersive, and full of wonder?


How might this kind of visual storytelling change the way students see learning?


In this section, we’ll explore how Google Veo is pushing the boundaries of AI-generated video. It’s designed not just for professional creators but also for teachers, students, and innovators who want to communicate complex ideas visually without needing cameras or editing software.


Veo’s capabilities go beyond static visuals; it captures the emotion and flow of a scene. It understands context like “a calm morning in a forest” versus “a stormy battlefield” and generates motion, color, and light that match your story’s tone.


This means that your lessons can now have cinematic storytelling power, your words become motion, your ideas become experiences.


​​Key Aspect: Advanced Capabilities

  • Cinematic Control: Users can specify camera angles ("close-up," "aerial shot," "tracking shot"), lighting ("golden hour," "moody lighting"), and visual styles ("photorealistic," "animated," "vintage film").

  • Image-to-Video: It can animate a static image, bringing source material like a diagram, historical photo, or AI-generated image to life.

  • Video Editing: It can extend existing video clips or create seamless transitions between different scenes.

  • Audio Generation: Newer versions can generate synchronized audio, sound effects, and even realistic dialogue based on the prompt.


So get ready to step into the future of visual learning, where teaching and creativity blend seamlessly into immersive storytelling powered by AI. 


Use Case: Visualizing the "Unseeable"

  • Concept: Make abstract, complex, or invisible concepts tangible.

  • Examples:

    • STEM: Show the process of protein folding, visualize a chemical reaction at the molecular level, or generate a simulation of a black hole.

    • Humanities: Create a video of an abstract philosophical concept, like "Plato's Cave."

    • Economics: Animate a graph to show supply and demand shifting in real-time.


Use Case: Dynamic Content & "Micro-Learning"

  • Examples:

    • All Disciplines: Generate a 60-second "explainer video" for a key term or theory.

    • Online Learning: Create visually interesting video introductions for weekly modules.

    • Marketing: (For the institution) Create short, dynamic videos for course promotion on social media.

  • Examples:

    • History: Generate a "realistic" short scene of a specific historical event, like a debate in the Roman Senate or a 1920s street scene.

    • Business: Create a short case study video depicting a difficult customer interaction or a boardroom negotiation.

    • Medicine/Nursing: Generate a video simulating the visual onset of specific patient symptoms.

  • Concept: Lower the technical barrier for students to create high-quality video-based assignments, focusing them on content and critical thinking instead of complex video editing.

  • Examples:

    • Literature: Students generate short "trailers" for the novel they are studying, capturing its tone and themes.

    • Languages: Students create a short, subtitled video dialogue between two AI-generated characters in the target language.

    • All Disciplines: As an alternative to a standard presentation, students create a 3-minute video essay or digital story illustrating their research.

AI for Audio: Text-to-Speech

How to Use Free Text-to-Speech Tools

Imagine if your lesson plan could speak to your students literally!


You type a few lines, click a button, and suddenly your text comes alive with a natural, clear, and expressive voice.


Welcome to the fascinating world of AI Text-to-Speech (TTS) where technology gives your words a voice. 


Tools like ElevenLabs, Play.ht, and Murf are revolutionizing how teachers share lessons, announcements, and reflections. You don’t need microphones, fancy studios, or recording experience, just your lesson notes and a spark of creativity.


Think:

  • Your lesson introduction could sound like a radio show.

  • Your weekly summary could be a warm, friendly voice reminding students of key takeaways.

  • Even your announcements could become mini audio messages that students can play anytime, anywhere.













You’ve just seen how incredibly simple it is to create professional-quality voice narrations from plain text.


No recording, no retakes, no equipment, just creativity and your message.


  • Step 1: Copy your text into a TTS platform (like ElevenLabs or Play.ht).


  • Step 2: Choose a voice male, female, friendly, formal, storyteller, or calm.



  • Step 3: Adjust tone and pacing to make your message fit your mood.

  • Step 4: Generate and download your audio instantly!


Notice how tone changes everything. A “calm” voice soothes. An “energetic” voice motivates. A “storytelling” voice captivates.


Voice is not just sound, it's emotion, personality, and engagement rolled into one.


Try This Experiment:

Take a paragraph from your lesson and create two versions: one calm, one enthusiastic.


Play both aloud and ask: “Which version would hold my students’ attention better?”


You’ll be amazed at how a few voice changes can turn plain text into an unforgettable learning experience.

steps.title: Create Audio Versions of Readings for Accessibility

content.htmlContent:
Create Audio Versions of Readings for Accessibility

Close your eyes and picture this scenario:

  • A student is walking to school, earbuds in, listening to your lesson on ecosystems while observing the trees along the way.

  • Another student, who struggles with reading, follows along with an AI-generated audio narration of your chapter notes.

  • A third student is revising for an exam by replaying your summary while cooking dinner or commuting home.


This is the magic of AI Text-to-Speech (TTS) for accessibility lessons that travel beyond the page, beyond the screen, and into everyday life.


AI audio tools like ElevenLabs, Play.ht, and Murf allow you to create high-quality audio versions of lessons, readings, or reflections instantly. These can be produced in multiple languages, accents, and tones, making learning inclusive for every student.


Audio versions help students with reading difficulties, visual impairments, or language barriers.


Having lessons in multiple languages or formats improves engagement and equity as well.


Accessibility isn’t just about convenience, it's about ensuring every student has the same opportunity to learn and succeed.


These tools can be used for:

  1. Creating Audio Versions Quickly: AI converts written lessons, summaries, or book chapters into clear, human-like narration within minutes.

  2. Language Inclusivity: Generate audio in English, regional languages, or multiple accents to support multilingual classrooms.

  3. Adjusting Tone and Style: Make your lesson sound friendly, engaging, and clear, not robotic or monotonous.


Real-World Use Cases for Educators:

  • Reading Support: Students with dyslexia or reading difficulties can follow along with audio narration.

  • Bilingual Learning: Lessons narrated in English and Hindi to aid comprehension in language-diverse classrooms.

  • Revision & Homework: Students can replay audio summaries while commuting or exercising.

  • Inclusive Projects: Visually impaired students can access narrated versions of textbooks or assignments.

  • Global Collaboration: Share audio lessons with partner classrooms worldwide  learning transcends geography.


AI audio tools make learning accessible, inclusive, and flexible. It’s more than convenience, it's a bridge connecting teachers and learners in powerful new ways.


Why This Matters?:

Audio versions of lessons:

  • Enhance engagement for students who prefer auditory learning.

  • Make lessons inclusive for students with learning challenges.

  • Extend learning beyond classroom walls.

  • Foster independent learning and revision.


Educators across the world are already using TTS to:

  • Narrate textbooks or chapter summaries for students with reading difficulties.

  • Produce bilingual audio clips for comprehension support.

  • Create downloadable lessons for offline access, especially in remote areas.

  • Add a friendly, human-like voice to lessons to make content more relatable.


Try This Real-World Task:

  1. Select a 2-page reading passage or a topic summary.

  2. Use ElevenLabs, Play.ht, or Murf to generate an audio version.

  3. Listen from a student’s perspective:

    • Is the tone engaging?

    • Is it clear and easy to understand?

    • Does it feel human, friendly, and lively?

  4. Create two versions: one in English, and another in your local language.

  5. Share it with your class and watch how students enjoy learning while on the move.


Reflection:

Ask yourself:

  • Could combining voiceovers, podcasts, and TTS narration create a full multimedia learning experience?

  • How might audio lessons transform learning for students with diverse needs?


With AI-generated audio, your lessons become truly universal  learning that moves, listens, and speaks to every student.

Generating Voiceovers for Presentations

Picture this: You open your PowerPoint or Google Slides, click “play,” and instead of reading the content aloud, a warm, engaging voice narrates each slide for you.


Your lesson instantly feels like a mini-documentary, complete with storytelling, pacing, and emphasis all without recording a single word yourself. 


That’s the magic of AI-generated voiceovers. They bring your slides to life, hold student attention, and give your lessons a professional, cinematic feel. Whether you’re in class, flipped teaching, or sharing materials online, voiceovers can turn static slides into immersive learning experiences.


Imagine

  • How would it sound if it could “talk” to your students?

  • Could a science lesson sound like a Discovery Channel feature?

  • Could a literature lesson feel like a storyteller reading a timeless tale?

  • Could historical events come alive with dramatic tone and emotion?

  • How would your students respond if your slides spoke directly to them?


Real-Time Use Cases for Educators:

  • Step-by-Step Guides: Math or coding lessons narrated with clear instructions so students can follow independently at home.

  • Storytelling Lessons: History teachers can narrate events with drama and suspense, turning timelines into compelling stories.

  • Language Learning: Voiceovers can demonstrate proper pronunciation, accents, and intonation for ESL or foreign language lessons.

  • Flipped Classroom: Students can review narrated presentations at home, ensuring more active learning during class.

  • Special Needs Inclusion: Audio narration can support learners with reading difficulties or visual impairments.


Why does this matter? 

Voiceovers don’t just add sound they add emotion, engagement, and clarity. Students are more likely to remember key points when they hear them spoken with emphasis, pacing, and personality.


Educators around the world are already using this technology to:

  • Narrate science experiments step-by-step for at-home practice.

  • Add story-style commentary to literature or history lessons.

  • Create pronunciation guides in multiple languages for global classrooms.

  • Turn existing slide decks into ready-to-share, student-friendly learning modules.


Try This Creative Challenge:

  1. Pick one of your old presentations, maybe your favorite topic.

  2. Write 1-2 lines of narration per slide, keeping it conversational.

  3. Use Play.ht, Murf, or ElevenLabs to generate your AI voiceover.

  4. Insert the audio into your slides, adjusting timing for transitions and animations.

  5. Add background music or subtle sound effects.

  6. Record a student-friendly introduction to welcome learners at the start.

  7. Turn narrated slides into flipped classroom videos, letting students review at their own pace.

  8. Play it back and notice how your slides come alive with sound.


Encourage students to create their own narrated slides, turning assignments into audio-visual projects.


Reflection:

  • Which lessons would benefit most from narration?

  • How could voiceovers help students retain information better?

  • Could AI voiceovers support students with different learning needs in your class?


When done right, AI-generated voiceovers turn presentations from static slides into immersive classroom experiences with a blend of storytelling, clarity, and professional polish.

Create Podcasts

Your students are on their way to school, earbuds in, smiling as they listen to your voice saying, “Good morning, learners! Welcome to Science Bites with Ms. Mehra today we’re uncovering the mysteries of black holes!” 


Or maybe it’s History on the Go with Mr. Rao, where the past comes alive in 5-minute audio journeys.


Your lessons are no longer confined to the classroom; they're traveling with your students, on the bus, at home, during a walk, or even while doing chores. You’ve just turned your class into a portable device that educates, entertains, and inspires. 


That’s the power of AI audio tools like ElevenLabs, Play.ht, and Murf AI. These tools make podcasting so easy that any teacher can become a podcast host with no recording studio, expensive mic, or editing expertise required.


All you need to do is: Write your script → Pick a voice → Add background music →  Publish your episode!


AI handles the rest  voice clarity, tone modulation, pacing, and even language translations.


  • If you had your own classroom podcast, what would you call it? (Something fun like “Curious Minds Radio” or “Math Talks Minute”)

  • What stories, reflections, or quick lessons would you share first?

  • How might your students respond if they could hear your lessons on their own time, not just read them?


AI-powered podcasting is more than just an audio trend, it's a way to connect.


Teachers around the world are using podcasts to:

  • Summarize lessons for revision and reflection.

  • Motivate students before exams with short pep talks.

  • Build deeper connections with families by sharing class updates and learning highlights.

  • Encourage language practice, where students listen and repeat pronunciation exercises.

  • Foster creativity, as students script and record their own podcast episodes on chosen topics.


With podcasting, your voice becomes a bridge between classroom learning and real-life engagement.


Now, your classroom can speak anytime, anywhere.


Here’s What You Can Do Right Away:

  • Transform your weekly summaries into short, engaging podcast recaps. (Example: “This Week in Science : Newton’s Laws in Real Life!”)

  • Record motivational reflections: a two-minute “Monday Motivation” or “Friday Recap.”

  • Empower students by letting them host episodes, using their real or AI-generated voices.

  • Create bilingual podcasts: one version in English, another in Hindi or another regional language, for inclusive learning.

  • Collaborate globally: exchange audio stories with classrooms across the world!


Your Turn- The Classroom Podcast Challenge!

Here’s your step-by-step creative challenge:


  1. Pick a topic you love teaching:  something simple yet powerful.

    Examples:

    • “Is the sky really Blue”

    • “The Power of Kindness”

    • “The Hidden Math in Music”


  2. Write your script: short and conversational 

    • Start with a greeting.

    • Introduce your topic in a storytelling way.

    • End with a reflection or a thought-provoking question.


  3. Choose your AI voice using platforms like ElevenLabs, Play.ht, or Murf AI.

    Teaching in multiple languages? Generate versions in Hindi, Spanish, or French!


  1. Name your show! Think of something catchy that fits your personality:

    • EduEchoes

    • The Learning Lounge

    • Classroom Chronicles 

    • Brainwaves with Ms. Marie


  2. Publish and share it!

    • Ask students to listen and comment on their favorite part.

M4 - Task 2

Create an AI-Powered Audio Resource

(Estimated Time: 90 minutes)


Objective: This task helps you move from understanding AI audio tools to applying them in your own teaching context. You’ll use AI to create a short multimedia resource; an audio version of a reading or lesson (using text-to-speech). By the end of this activity, you’ll experience firsthand how AI can save preparation time, improve accessibility, and add a professional edge to your teaching materials.


Create an Accessible Reading or Lesson (Using Text-to-Speech Tool)

You will create an audio version of a written resource, such as a short article, essay, or reading passage, to make your learning content more inclusive and accessible.


Instructions:


Step 1: Select a Reading (Approx. 2 Pages)

Choose a reading or excerpt relevant to your course. For instance:

  • A research summary in Psychology of Learning

  • An article from Harvard Business Review

  • A passage from a History textbook


Step 2: Prepare the Text

  • Clean up any formatting (remove citations, bullet points, or footnotes).

  • Make sure it reads naturally as spoken text.

    Tip: Add simple transitions like “In this section, we’ll explore…”


Step 3: Choose a Text-to-Speech Tool

Use one of the following (free or freemium):

  • ElevenLabs 

  • Natural Reader


Step 4: Generate the Audio

  • Paste your text into the tool.

  • Choose a clear, neutral, and gender-balanced voice.

  • Select the desired language or accent (e.g., British English, Indian English, American English).

  • Preview the clip and check pronunciation of key terms.


Step 5: Download and Test

  • Download your final audio file (MP3 or WAV).

  • Listen on headphones. Does it sound smooth and easy to follow?

  • Ensure the pace is appropriate for student comprehension.


Step 6: Optional Enhancements

  • Add a short intro like: “This is your Week 2 Reading on Motivation Theories.”

  • If using LMS, embed the audio file next to the text reading.


Checklist 

  • I selected a real classroom topic or reading relevant to my course.

  • I wrote a short script or cleaned up a 2-page reading for AI generation.

  • I used an AI tool (Synthesia, ElevenLabs, etc.) to generate audio.

  • I reviewed and refined tone, pacing, and clarity.

  • I downloaded the final file and evaluated how it could enhance student learning.


Self-Reflection Questions

Reflect on the experience in your notes or course journal:

  1. How did using AI for audio creation change your approach to content delivery?

  2. What challenges did you face in maintaining an authentic teacher voice through AI?

  3. In what ways could AI-generated audio make your lessons more inclusive, engaging, or efficient?

  4. Would you consider using AI to co-create learning materials with your students (e.g., student podcasts)?


Completion

Once you’ve completed your audio, review it one last time and upload or save it to your portfolio or LMS folder.


When done, mark this activity as “Complete” in your LMS.
